programmes.show_description: BSA Bikes/Athena/VW Beetle: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all.
Daf 66 Marathon Coupe: A vivid Dutch DAF 66 - in daffodil yellow - lands at the North Yorkshire showroom.
Jaguar 2+2 E Type: Paul's on a big day out collecting a 1971 Jaguar E-Type and a 1933 Morgan 3-wheeler Supersport but first there's a flock of 300 chickens to negotiate before he can load up.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Unfinished Portrait: Lucy Worsley examines the author's later life and discovers how personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.
Shopping from Home.
Ulverston to Ravenglass: Michael Portillo travels though Cumbria, setting sail in a small craft on Lake Windermere.
St Bees to Maryport: Michael Portillo embarks on the last leg of his railway journey around the north-west coast of England, visiting the childhood home of William Wordsworth.
Middlesborough to Boulby: Michael Portillo hunts for dark matter in the deepest mine in Britain in Boulby, North Yorkshire, then visits Middlesbrough's Transporter Bridge.
Whitby to Robin Hood's Bay: Michael Portillo travels from Whitby, where he explores the port's shipbuilding and seafaring heritage, to Robin Hood's Bay, a former smuggling haven.
Subaru Impreza: Dave's in fast car heaven as he picks up a rare Subaru Impreza and an equally powerful Ford Escort RS Cosworth from North Wales.
Ford Granada: A rare 1968 Honda N600 car emerges from a lock up in County Durham and Derek's in Scotland to collect an original RAC Bedford van.
The First Giants: The First World War led to a number of astounding war factories, which laid the foundations and paved the way for modern factories of today.
Stalingrad: The Nazis have lost in Moscow and Hitler and Stalin now take charge of their armies - with no-one to hold them back, Hitler has met his match in a fight to the death.
Media City UK 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show.
Eltham Palace 2: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Eltham Palace, where items brought in to be valued include a panel of stained glass from the Houses of Parliament.
Ford Transit 100/Yugo Zastava: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isn't all that it seems.

Vincent Bike/Renault 4CV: Paul is off to the Lake District for a poignant collection from a Mathewsons regular - with seven vehicles to load including a Thames Truck, a Zodiac and a Vauxhall Victor. S

In the fifth and final leg of his journey, Simon travels across Central America from the Caribbean to the Pacific by beginning in Belize and finishing his trip in Costa Rica.

Tesla Model X/Showman's Caravan: Paul, Mick and Charlie are in Hull for one of their biggest ever collections. Dave takes a test drive in Tesla Model X and Derek is bowled over by a classic vintage mo
The gold hunters battle wild outback weather near key landmarks that hopefully point to the legendary fortune. A new team member brings equipment, expertise and a different perspective.
Ford Transit 100/Yugo Zastava: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isn't all that it seems.

programmes.show_name: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: The American Revolution: Lucy Worsley explores how American history has been mythologised and manipulated - beginning with the American Revolution.
Shopping from Home.
Scarborough to Bridlington: Michael Portillo is in the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Scarborough to explore one of the greatest royal fortresses in England.
Hornsea to Spurn Point: Michael Portillo travels from the seaside resort of Hornsea, to Spurn Point, where he learns about a life-saving 19th-century invention.
Grimsby to Humberston Fitties: Michael Portillo reaches Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the town's history.
Ballarena to Giant's Causeway: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Ireland's Causeway coast beginning a new journey along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster.
Nash Metropolitan: Derek collects five beautiful veteran motorbikes from an enthusiast who's still happily riding in his 90s.
Ford Capri Consort: A rare 1964 Ford Capri Consul coupe comes up for auction. Paul takes a 1957 Leyland Comet truck for a spin, and Dave admits that high on his wish-list is a Citroen 2CV.
Rolls-Royce: The Spitfire, the Hurricane, the Lancaster and Wellington Bombers, and the P51 Mustang were all powered by the Rolls-Royce Merlin engine.
The Home Front: Facing defeat on the Eastern Front, Hitler elects to retreat to Germany - deep rifts emerge, his inner circle vies for power, and an enemy within plots to kill Hitler.
Crathes Castle 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire, where the items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems.
Aerospace Bristol 2: Fiona Bruce presents from underneath the Concorde at Aerospace in Bristol, where objects brought in to be valued include a Fijian war club and a tiny Chinese vase.
Rover 213 S/TR8/E-Type: Derek's running late as usual, to collect a Triumph TR8 from Kirkby Stephen East Railway Station, with vintage engine ride thrown in for good measure.
MG/GN Racecars/Honda Prelude: Dave collects a barely used 1984 Honda Prelude from one of his all-time heroes - motorcycle legend, Ron Haslam.
Rolls-Royce: The Spitfire, the Hurricane, the Lancaster and Wellington Bombers, and the P51 Mustang were all powered by the Rolls-Royce Merlin engine.
The Home Front: Facing defeat on the Eastern Front, Hitler elects to retreat to Germany - deep rifts emerge, his inner circle vies for power, and an enemy within plots to kill Hitler.
Crathes Castle 1: Fiona Bruce presents from Crathes Castle in Aberdeenshire, where the items brought in to be valued include a royal portrait which is not all that it seems.
Simon Reeve journeys through the remote northeast of South America from the spectacular Mount Roraima in Venezuela through Guyana, Suriname, and French Guiana.
The Gestapo Ruins a Picnic: 1980s sitcom that finds farcical fun in the French Resistance. Von Klinkerhoffen wants to use the local newspaper for Nazi propaganda and asks Rene to run it.
The Spirit of Nouvion: Another merry trip to wartime France. Michelle enlists Rene to try to retrieve the Fallen Madonna locked in Herr Flick's safe. What could possibly go wrong!?
Leg it to Spain: Farcical sitcom with Gorden Kaye as amorous cafe owner Rene Artois. Those bumbling British airmen are trapped in wine barrels in the cellars of the chateau.
Rover 213 S/TR8/E-Type: Derek's running late as usual, to collect a Triumph TR8 from Kirkby Stephen East Railway Station, with vintage engine ride thrown in for good measure.
